output_rules.purpose: Generate an evidence-gap search plan that verifies the weakest links in top-journal-level scientific review.
do_not_output: bare keyword lists without a claim being tested
generic search terms such as 'more controls' or 'recent review' without source type and decision relevance
preprint-only searches for decisive criticisms when peer-reviewed, benchmark, guideline, structure, theorem, clinical, or artifact evidence is available

hint_template.evidence_gap_or_claim_tested: Name the exact unsupported claim, missing control, characterization gap, benchmark weakness, proof gap, clinical validation gap, alternative explanation, or interdisciplinary bridge uncertainty.
hint_template.preferred_sources: Specify primary papers, method benchmarks, official reporting guidelines, clinical trial records, public datasets, spectra/structure repositories, code artifacts, theorem sources, standards, or preprints as frontier-only signals.
hint_template.decision_changing_evidence: State what result would strengthen, weaken, or force narrowing of the review conclusion.
hint_template.linked_issue_or_revision_action: Map the search to a specific Critical/Major issue or required revision action.

evidence_gap_categories: controls, baselines, characterizations, proof assumptions, or validation cohorts
statistical, causal, proof, measurement, or clinical adequacy
reproducibility / data, code, materials, spectra, structures, proofs, and auditability
contrary literature, contrary theorem, failed replication, or contrary benchmark
article_specific_dossier: google scholar: <field term> landmark canonical theory method
google scholar: <method/source type> reporting guideline benchmark validation
google scholar: <field term> recent 3 years controversy review
publisher or database: <exact claim or method> citation chain

EQUATOR CONSORT PRISMA STROBE ARRIVE SPIRIT TRIPOD reporting guideline
provisional_frontier: check posted date, version history, peer-reviewed publication link, replication status, and whether conclusions are independent or confirmatory

model_output_examples.evidence_gap_or_claim_tested: Whether an AI model's claimed performance gain survives leakage-free evaluation against current baselines.
model_output_examples.preferred_sources: Benchmark papers, baseline implementations, ACM artifact guidance, NeurIPS/ICLR reproducibility guidance, and independent replications.
search_strings: ACM artifact review reproducibility benchmark machine learning
model_output_examples.decision_changing_evidence: Independent leakage-free benchmarks showing the same gain would strengthen the claim; leakage reports or failed replications would force claim narrowing.
model_output_examples.linked_issue_or_revision_action: Critical issue: benchmark validity and reproducibility.

model_output_examples.evidence_gap_or_claim_tested: Whether a new chemical probe's biological phenotype is due to the claimed target rather than impurity or off-target activity.
model_output_examples.preferred_sources: ACS characterization guidance, primary target-engagement papers, orthogonal assay papers, and medicinal chemistry selectivity benchmarks.
search_strings: ACS <compound class> characterization purity NMR HRMS
pubmed: <target> chemical probe target engagement selectivity off target
google scholar: <assay> orthogonal validation chemical biology
model_output_examples.decision_changing_evidence: Complete characterization plus orthogonal target engagement would strengthen the claim; missing purity or selectivity would require narrowing to a candidate probe.
model_output_examples.linked_issue_or_revision_action: Major issue: chemical identity and biological specificity.

model_output_examples.evidence_gap_or_claim_tested: Whether a theorem's assumptions exclude a boundary case that would invalidate the claimed generality.
model_output_examples.preferred_sources: MathSciNet/zbMATH records, prior theorem sources, counterexample papers, and canonical monographs.
model_output_examples.decision_changing_evidence: A known counterexample would force theorem narrowing; a prior theorem with identical assumptions would reduce novelty.
model_output_examples.linked_issue_or_revision_action: Critical issue: proof assumptions and novelty.
